128: static int isoptish(const char* text) {

132: static int isoption(const char* text, char shortopt, const char* longopt, size_t *short_opts) {
133:   if (text[0] != '-' || text[1] == '-')
134:     *short_opts = 0;
135:   if (text[0] != '-') return 0;
136: 
137:   // check long option
138:   if (text[1] == '-' && !strcmp(text+2, longopt)) return 1;
139:   else if (text[1] == '-') return 0;
140: 
141:   // must be short option; check it and...
142:   if (!shortopt) return 0;
143:   if (strchr(text, shortopt) != NULL) {
144:     (*short_opts)++; // ...count it (for option stacking)
145:     return 1;
146:   }
147:   return 0;
148: }

<source_slice path="docs/content/manual/manual.yml" lines="272-319">
272:       * `--args`:
273: 
274:         Remaining arguments are positional string arguments.  These are
275:         available to the jq program as `$ARGS.positional[]`.
276: 
277:       * `--jsonargs`:
278: 
279:         Remaining arguments are positional JSON text arguments.  These
280:         are available to the jq program as `$ARGS.positional[]`.
281: 
282:       * `--exit-status` / `-e`:
283: 
284:         Sets the exit status of jq to 0 if the last output value was
285:         neither `false` nor `null`, 1 if the last output value was
286:         either `false` or `null`, or 4 if no valid result was ever
287:         produced.  Normally jq exits with 2 if there was any usage
288:         problem or system error, 3 if there was a jq program compile
289:         error, or 0 if the jq program ran.
290: 
291:         Another way to set the exit status is with the `halt_error`
292:         builtin function.
293: 
294:       * `--binary` / `-b`:
295: 
296:         Windows users using WSL, MSYS2, or Cygwin, should use this option
297:         when using a native jq.exe, otherwise jq will turn newlines (LFs)
298:         into carriage-return-then-newline (CRLF).
299: 
300:       * `--version` / `-V`:
301: 
302:         Output the jq version and exit with zero.
303: 
304:       * `--build-configuration`:
305: 
306:         Output the build configuration of jq and exit with zero.
307:         This output has no supported format or structure and may change
308:         without notice in future releases.
309: 
310:       * `--help` / `-h`:
311: 
312:         Output the jq help and exit with zero.
313: 
314:       * `--`:
315: 
316:         Terminates argument processing.  Remaining arguments are
317:         positional, either strings, JSON texts, or input filenames,
318:         according to whether `--args` or `--jsonargs` were given.
319:

132: static int isoption(const char* text, char shortopt, const char* longopt, size_t *short_opts) {
133:   if (text[0] != '-' || text[1] == '-')
134:     *short_opts = 0;
135:   if (text[0] != '-') return 0;
136: 
137:   // check long option
138:   if (text[1] == '-' && !strcmp(text+2, longopt)) return 1;
139:   else if (text[1] == '-') return 0;
140: 
141:   // must be short option; check it and...
142:   if (!shortopt) return 0;
143:   if (strchr(text, shortopt) != NULL) {
144:     (*short_opts)++; // ...count it (for option stacking)
145:     return 1;
146:   }
147:   return 0;
148: }

132: static int isoption(const char* text, char shortopt, const char* longopt, size_t *short_opts) {
133:   if (text[0] != '-' || text[1] == '-')
134:     *short_opts = 0;
135:   if (text[0] != '-') return 0;
136: 
137:   // check long option
138:   if (text[1] == '-' && !strcmp(text+2, longopt)) return 1;
139:   else if (text[1] == '-') return 0;
140: 
141:   // must be short option; check it and...
142:   if (!shortopt) return 0;
143:   if (strchr(text, shortopt) != NULL) {
144:     (*short_opts)++; // ...count it (for option stacking)
145:     return 1;
146:   }
147:   return 0;
148: }
